body:
## 🤖 AI-Powered Solution

This PR implements the `--auto-start-screen-watch-message` option for hive-telegram-bot as requested in issue #467.

### 📋 Issue Reference
Fixes #467

### ✅ Implementation Details

#### Core Features

1. **Real-time Terminal Monitoring**
   - Watches screen sessions and updates Telegram messages with live terminal output
   - Updates every 2.5 seconds (rate-limited to respect Telegram API)
   - Only updates when actual screen content changes
   - Displays last 25 lines of terminal output in code block format

2. **Smart Completion Handling**
   - Automatically detects when screen session ends
   - Freezes the message with final output
   - Attempts to attach full log file as document
   - Shows completion status in message

3. **Security & Privacy**
   - **Automatically disabled for private repositories**
   - Checks repository visibility using `gh` CLI
   - Fails safely if visibility cannot be determined
   - Off by default (opt-in with `--auto-start-screen-watch-message`)

#### New Files

- **`src/screen-watcher.lib.mjs`**: Core monitoring module
  - `isScreenSessionRunning()`: Check if screen session exists
  - `getScreenOutput()`: Capture screen content using hardcopy
  - `getLastLines()`: Extract last N lines from output
  - `formatOutputForTelegram()`: Format for Telegram with code blocks
  - `watchScreenSession()`: Main watcher with async updates
  - `findLogFile()`: Locate log files for attachment

#### Modified Files

- **`src/telegram-bot.mjs`**:
  - Added `--auto-start-screen-watch-message` CLI option
  - Integrated watcher into `/solve` command handler
  - Added `isRepoPrivate()` helper function for visibility checks
  - Imported screen-watcher module functions

- **`README.md`**:
  - Added documentation for new bot option
  - Added "Bot Options" section with all available flags
  - Added "Live Terminal Watch" feature description
  - Updated feature list

### 🔧 Technical Approach

The implementation uses GNU Screen's `hardcopy` command to efficiently capture terminal output without attaching to the session. This approach:
- Doesn't interfere with running commands
- Provides snapshot of current screen content
- Works reliably across different terminal states
- Minimizes resource usage

### 🎯 Usage

```bash
# Start bot with live terminal watch enabled
hive-telegram-bot --token YOUR_TOKEN --auto-start-screen-watch-message

# Or use environment variable
export TELEGRAM_AUTO_WATCH_MESSAGE=true
hive-telegram-bot --token YOUR_TOKEN
```

### 📸 Behavior

1. User sends `/solve https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123` in Telegram group
2. Bot starts screen session and confirms
3. Bot creates additional message: "🔄 Initializing live terminal watch..."
4. Message updates every 2.5s with latest terminal output (last 25 lines)
5. When session completes:
   - Message updated to show "✅ Terminal Session Completed"
   - Final output displayed
   - Full log file attached (if found)

### 🔒 Security Considerations

- **Private repos**: Feature automatically disabled
- **Unknown visibility**: Feature automatically disabled (fail-safe)
- **Rate limiting**: Updates respect Telegram API limits (min 1.5s between updates)
- **Output truncation**: Large output truncated to fit Telegram's 4096 char limit

### 📚 Inspiration

Based on the proof-of-concept from [konard/telegram-terminal-bot](https://github.com/konard/telegram-terminal-bot), adapted for Hive Mind's architecture.

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
